questions.q: Rani Padmini is the legendary queen associated with which fort?
questions.explanation: Padmini, or Padmavati, is the legendary queen of Chittor in present-day Rajasthan.

questions.q: By what other name is Rani Padmini also widely known?
questions.explanation: Rani Padmini is also known as Padmavati in legend and literature.

questions.q: The siege of Chittor linked to Rani Padmini was led by which Sultan?
questions.explanation: The siege of Chittor associated with Padmini's legend was led by Alauddin Khalji of Delhi.

questions.q: Around which year is the siege of Chittor connected with Rani Padmini traditionally dated?
questions.explanation: The siege of Chittor by Alauddin Khalji is traditionally dated to around 1303 CE.

questions.q: Rani Padmini's story is best described as a blend of what?
questions.explanation: Padmini's story is woven from history and legend, and her historicity is debated by scholars.

questions.q: The famous later poem that immortalised Padmini's story is called what?
questions.explanation: Padmini's legend was immortalised in the epic poem Padmavat, written long after the events.

questions.q: Chittor, the fort tied to Padmini, was a stronghold of which Rajput line?
questions.explanation: Chittor was long the capital of the Guhila/Sisodia rulers of Mewar.

questions.q: Rani Padmini is celebrated in legend chiefly for which quality?
questions.explanation: Padmini is celebrated in legend especially for her extraordinary beauty and her defence of honour.

questions.q: The events of Padmini's legend belong to which broad period of Indian history?
questions.explanation: The legend is set during the Delhi Sultanate period, in the reign of Alauddin Khalji.

questions.q: Chittor fort, central to Padmini's legend, is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh, the fort of Padmini's legend, lies in present-day Rajasthan.

questions.q: According to legend, Padmini was the wife of which Rajput ruler of Chittor?
questions.explanation: In the legend, Padmini was the queen of Rana Ratan Singh (Ratnasimha) of Chittor.

questions.q: Who composed the Sufi epic poem Padmavat that gave Padmini's story lasting fame?
questions.explanation: The poem Padmavat was composed by Malik Muhammad Jayasi in the 16th century, more than two centuries after the siege.

questions.q: In roughly which century was Jayasi's Padmavat composed, well after the events it describes?
questions.explanation: Jayasi composed Padmavat in the 16th century CE, long after the early-14th-century siege.

questions.q: In which language did Malik Muhammad Jayasi compose the Padmavat?
questions.explanation: Jayasi wrote the Padmavat in the Awadhi dialect of Hindi, using Persian script.

questions.q: The collective self-immolation of women said to have occurred during the fall of Chittor is known as what?
questions.explanation: The mass self-immolation by the women of Chittor to avoid capture is known as jauhar in Rajput tradition.

questions.q: Which contemporary court historian recorded Alauddin Khalji's actual capture of Chittor in 1303?
questions.explanation: Amir Khusrau, who accompanied Alauddin, recorded the historical capture of Chittor in 1303 but does not mention Padmini.

questions.q: What name did Alauddin Khalji reportedly give to Chittor after capturing it, naming it for his son?
questions.explanation: Alauddin renamed Chittor 'Khizrabad' after his son Khizr Khan, according to contemporary accounts.

questions.q: Most modern historians regard Padmini herself as which of the following?
options: A figure whose historicity is uncertain
questions.explanation: Most historians treat Padmini as a legendary or semi-legendary figure whose historical existence is uncertain.

questions.q: Chittor, the setting of Padmini's legend, was the capital of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Chittor was the capital of the kingdom of Mewar in Rajputana.

questions.q: In Jayasi's poem, Padmini is described as a princess originally from where?
questions.explanation: In Jayasi's Padmavat, Padmini is portrayed as a princess from Singhal, identified with Sri Lanka.

questions.q: The 1303 capture of Chittor was one episode in Alauddin Khalji's broader campaign against whom?
questions.explanation: The capture of Chittor was part of Alauddin Khalji's campaigns to subdue the Rajput kingdoms of western India.

questions.q: Why do historians treat the romantic details of Padmini's story with caution?
options: They were carved on Ashokan pillars
They appear only in much later literary sources
They survive only in Chola inscriptions
questions.explanation: The romantic narrative around Padmini appears only in literary works composed long after 1303, not in contemporary records.

questions.q: In Jayasi's Padmavat, who is said to have betrayed Ratan Singh by informing Alauddin of Padmini's beauty?
questions.explanation: In the poem, the exiled brahmin Raghav Chetan tells Alauddin of Padmini's beauty, setting the tragedy in motion.

questions.q: According to the legend, by what means did Alauddin first glimpse Padmini?
options: In a painted portrait sent to him
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Alauddin was allowed only to see Padmini's reflection in a mirror, not the queen directly.

questions.q: The 1303 jauhar at Chittor is traditionally counted as which in the fort's series of such acts?
questions.explanation: The jauhar of 1303 is remembered as the first of the three great jauhars in the history of Chittor.

questions.q: Which dynasty did the Rana of Chittor in Padmini's legend belong to, a branch of the Guhilas?
options: The Rawal branch of the Guhilas
questions.explanation: Ratan Singh of the legend belonged to the Rawal line of the Guhila clan that ruled Chittor before the Sisodia branch.

questions.q: Jayasi's Padmavat is widely read as which kind of work beyond a historical romance?
questions.explanation: Padmavat is understood as a Sufi allegory, with Padmini's beauty symbolising divine truth sought by the soul.

questions.q: Contemporary sources confirm that after taking Chittor, Alauddin entrusted it to whom before later Rajput recovery?
questions.explanation: Historical accounts say Alauddin placed Chittor under his young son Khizr Khan after its capture in 1303.

questions.q: Which later Rajput chronicler-tradition helped popularise Padmini's tale within Rajput memory?
options: The bardic and Rajput chronicles compiled over later centuries
questions.explanation: Padmini's story spread through later Rajput bardic chronicles and traditions, reinforcing it as part of Mewar's heroic memory.

questions.q: What is the scholarly consensus on whether contemporary 1303 sources name Padmini?
options: Amir Khusrau names her in detail
questions.explanation: No contemporary source from 1303, including Amir Khusrau's account, names Padmini; she first appears in literature two centuries later.
